mentions.note: Up 3% YTD, continuing magical run from Q4 2025; owned for Investing Club trust; breaking free from MAG7 weakness.
Broke free from mega-cap tech group in Q4 to soar; continued magical run, up 3% to start 2026; owns travel trust; only bright spot in mega-cap tech rotation.
Gemini 3 regarded as best-in-class generative AI; solved search-cannibalization concern; gaining enterprise and consumer traction vs. OpenAI.
Only Magnificent 7 stock currently strong; Cramer expects money to flow back to most of the seven eventually because they have too many levers and too much capital to bet against.
Announced own chips but remains gigantic Nvidia customer. Demand from hyperscalers (Google, Amazon, Microsoft) enormous and growing — validates Nvidia's secular position.

Best stock in the business by Cramer's count; many wrote off as worst Magnificent Seven but Gemini proved superior; owns YouTube, Waymo self-driving, Google Search—multiple competitive moats; recent buyback into Charitable Trust.

22% revenue growth, 82% EPS growth, Google Cloud +63% to $20B, custom TPU demand strong, Gemini Enterprise paid users +40% QoQ, raising CapEx by $5B to $180–190B with more expected in 2027; coining money, clear line of sight to profitability.
Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Original FANG stock (Google); proven secular growth leader with rate-proof fundamentals; dominates search and cloud computing.
Important AI capabilities; mentioned as context for tech dominance but not specifically recommended for action.

Multiple revenue streams: YouTube (largest video company), Waymo (number-one self-driving), Google/Gemini search, Google Cloud (best horse to bet on); higher-priced multiple justified by diversification.
Owns chips but Cramer likes all three (Nvidia, Amazon, Alphabet) for opportunity scale; not Jensen's preference but still major AI customer; deep pockets to invest.
Shock $80B capital raise signals data center buildout severely underestimated costs; vulnerability in AI capex arms race.
Successfully raised $45B of planned $85B secondary offering despite market volatility; Berkshire Hathaway's $10B participation validates strong execution by Goldman Sachs.
Successfully raised $45B secondary offering with Goldman Sachs underwriting; huge demand, few flippers; stock bought back $45B last year, now selling $15B—capital deployment shift.
Just completed multi-billion fund raise for AI. Supply wave will impact stock in near term as investors raise cash.

Equity issuance to fund unprofitable data-center buildout raises dilution fears. Though $920M/month SpaceX deal shows Google Cloud customers prefer Nvidia GPUs over internal TPUs—a bullish signal for Nvidia offset by equity-supply concerns.
Apple's AI deal with Alphabet makes Google the default search engine and extends Gemini integration. Neutral to positive for Alphabet as a beneficiary of AI deals.
Down 6% despite strong YouTube, Google Cloud and search performance, overshadowed by capex-driven free-cash-flow worries.
Best-performing Mag 7 stock of 2025 on Gemini 3 strength and fading antitrust risk; Cramer is rebuilding a big position.
Increasingly looks like the only clear AI winner, aided by its Apple Siri partnership.
Winning AI race on consumer side (Gemini); best-in-class at everything (Google, YouTube, Cloud); $175-185B capex forecast justified.
Only Mag 7 member besides Nvidia beating market; up 68%; synonymous with beat-and-raise; Waymo raises $16B at $126B valuation; something good happens every minute.
Announced memory-killer technology but stock down on announcement—lack of conviction suggests claim unproven; memory stocks' selloff not justified.
Data center buildout critical for AI; okay showing today but rally lacked follow-through.
Negative whispers about Google/Gemini losing to Anthropic/ChatGPT; Cramer started buying for travel trust; YouTube, Waymo, search, Chrome, Gemini all strong; hold through pessimism.
Hardware user capitalizing on AI boom; G in FANG; positioned with Amazon and Meta in hardware/AI winning camp.
Eric's portfolio; crushing it; data center, YouTube, everywhere; one of absolute favorite holdings.
100X data suggests Google Bard/Gemini at risk from Claude; similar to Google search vs. competitors in early days; AI momentum favors strong execution.
Google Cloud infrastructure layer; fourth-layer AI model (Google Superintelligence); key ecosystem player.
Strong hold; caller up $60 from $275; Cramer says sellers will lose, heading to $400; best growth/profitability story with durable moat.
Mosaic of revenue: Google search, Gemini, YouTube, Wemo, Google Cloud; numbers could be outstanding; Google Cloud particularly important.
Mega-cap tech earnings tomorrow; past peaks before earnings have led to selloffs; watch guidance on capex spend. Held in trust.
Crushed Q1 in every line; Google Cloud extraordinary, YouTube and Search incredibly strong, funding semiconductor buildout; one of two winners in Magnificent Seven.
Securing extraordinary returns through massive data center capex; Anthropic $200B chip/cloud deal soaring stock after-hours.
Major model builder spending insane amounts on AI infrastructure; part of AI revolution layer 4.
